description: Session Detail & Project Page Improvements - Collapse turns by default, truncate verbose content, filter memory to session-used files, and add Memory/Skills tabs to the project page

userStories.title: Collapse turns by default except the last turn
userStories.description: As a user viewing a session, I want only the last turn expanded so I can quickly see the most recent activity without scrolling through a wall of content.
acceptanceCriteria: ConversationTimeline passes defaultExpanded={false} to all TurnCards except the last, which gets defaultExpanded={true}
Collapsed turns show the existing 2-line preview text (via getPreviewText())
The last turn renders fully expanded
Expand all / Collapse all button still works as before
Manually expanding/collapsing individual turns still works
When Show all loads remaining turns beyond first 30, default collapse behavior applies to newly loaded turns as well
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Truncate expanded turn detail content with View button
userStories.description: As a user, I want expanded turns to show only the first 3 lines of detailed content with a View button to expand, so turns don't take up excessive vertical space.
acceptanceCriteria: When a turn is expanded, long content blocks (tool input details, lengthy assistant text) show only the first 3 lines using CSS line-clamp or equivalent
A View button appears below truncated content to expand it fully
Clicking View reveals full content inline and button text changes to Hide
Short content (3 lines or fewer) displays fully without a View button
This applies to tool input/detail sections within TurnCard events
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Backend: filter session memory to only referenced files
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need the session memory endpoints to return only memory files actually used in the session, not all project memory files.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse session JSONL to extract memory file names referenced in system prompts or tool calls (look for memory/ path patterns in message content)
Backend endpoint /ws/sessions/{sessionId}/memory returns only session-relevant memory files
REST endpoint /api/sessions/{sessionId}/memory also returns only session-relevant memory files
Returns empty array if no memory files were used in the session
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend: update Memory sidebar to show session-used files only
userStories.description: As a user viewing a session's memory, I want to see only the memory files that were actually referenced during that session.
acceptanceCriteria: MemorySidebar.tsx displays only memory files returned by the updated backend endpoint (session-relevant files)
If no memory files were used in the session, show an empty state message like 'No memory files used in this session'
Existing expand/collapse and markdown rendering behavior unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend: add project-level memory endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a REST endpoint to fetch all memory files for a project, not scoped to a session.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint GET /api/projects/{project_path}/memory returns all .md files from the project's memory directory (~/.claude/projects/{projectDir}/memory/)
Response format: array of MemoryFile objects (name + content), same as existing session memory endpoint
Returns empty array if directory doesn't exist or has no .md files
project_path is URL-encoded project directory path
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse existing load_memory_files() logic without session-scoping

userStories.title: Backend: add project-level skills listing endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a REST endpoint to list all available skills for a project across project, global, and plugin sources.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint GET /api/projects/{project_path}/skills returns all skills
Scans: {project_cwd}/.claude/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/, and plugin skill directories
Each skill includes: name, content (markdown), source ('project' | 'user' | 'plugin'), path
Deduplicates by name: project overrides global overrides plugin
Returns empty array if no skills found
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extend existing skill resolution logic from app.py to list all instead of resolving one by name

userStories.title: Frontend: add Memory tab to project page
userStories.description: As a user viewing a project, I want a Memory tab that lists all memory files so I can browse and read them.
acceptanceCriteria: New Memory tab appears in the project page tab bar after Sessions and Insights
Tab fetches data from GET /api/projects/{project_path}/memory
Lists all .md files with their names as clickable list items
Clicking a memory file opens a side panel (same style as existing MemorySidebar) showing full markdown content
Side panel can be closed via X button or clicking outside
Loading state shown while fetching memory files
Empty state message if no memory files exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend: add Skills tab to project page
userStories.description: As a user viewing a project, I want a Skills tab that lists all available skills so I can browse and read them.
acceptanceCriteria: New Skills tab appears in the project page tab bar after Memory
Tab fetches data from GET /api/projects/{project_path}/skills
Lists all skills with name and source label (project / user / plugin)
Clicking a skill opens a side panel (same style as existing SkillPanel) showing skill markdown content
Side panel can be closed via X button or clicking outside
Loading state shown while fetching skills
Empty state message if no skills exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Release: build and verify with Make command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to build the project using the Make command, run it, and verify everything works before releasing.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `make build` successfully with no errors
Run `make run` and verify the app starts on :3333
Verify frontend loads and sessions display correctly
Bump version if needed
userStories.notes: Final release task - run make build && make run to verify
